mnemonics.title: Cache Mapping Types
mnemonics.content: D-I-R: Direct = Index, Fully = Anywhere, Set-associative = Index + Way

mnemonics.title: Locality Principles
mnemonics.content: T-S: Temporal = Same location again, Spatial = Nearby location

mnemonics.title: Write Policies
mnemonics.content: W-T-W-B: Write-Through = Always write to memory, Write-Back = Write to cache first

mnemonics.title: MMU Functions
mnemonics.content: A-P-V: Address translation, Protection, Virtual memory support

acronyms.usage: Hardware cache for virtual-to-physical address translations
Hardware component handling memory protection and virtual addressing
Cache replacement policy evicts least recently accessed block
Cache replacement policy evicts oldest block
Cache replacement policy evicts least accessed block
Cache coherence protocol for multiprocessor systems

visualTips.visualization: Draw cache as a 2D table: rows=sets, columns=ways. Each cell shows tag, data, valid/dirty bits. Show how address divides into tag/index/offset fields.
Think of virtual address as chapter number (page) and page offset as line number. Physical memory is like a book where you can read any chapter from any shelf location.
Picture TLB as a dictionary: key = virtual page number, value = physical frame number. Quick lookup before consulting larger page table.
Use decision tree: CPU request → check cache tag → (hit) return data OR (miss) fetch from memory → update cache → return data

analogies.analogy: Like a refrigerator in your kitchen - frequently used items (milk, butter) kept close for quick access rather than going to the basement pantry each time.
Like a library's interlibrary loan system - you can access more books than physically available in your local branch by requesting from other locations.
Like a table of contents bookmark in a textbook - instead of flipping through every page to find a topic, you use the quick reference.
Like a library catalog system - tells you exactly which shelf (physical frame) contains the book (page) you want.
Fully associative = any parking spot, Direct = assigned numbered spot, Set-associative = zone with multiple spots.
